PLAINTIFF CHRISTINE LOPEZ' MOTION TO TRANSFER.

Plaintiff Christine Lopez's motion to transfer actions from other counties and coordinate them for all pretrial purposes is denied. Assuming without deciding that this action and the four others which are the subject of this motion are not complex and thus a permissible motion per Code of Civil Procedure 403, Ms. Lopez has failed to show that the factors
specified in Rule 3.500(d) favor her extraordinary request. The primary reasons that Ms. Lopez appears to advance to support her request that all five actions be coordinated in San Francisco for all pretrial purposes is that this Court has a civil master calendar, as opposed to direct civil calendars, and this Court has dedicated law and motion departments for non-complex to hear certain pretrial motions in cases not designated complex. While arguably these reasons relate to the factor stated in 3.500(d)(5) ("the efficient utilization of judicial facilities and staff resources"), this Court does not and will not take the position that its manner of handling civil cases or pretrial portions thereof is more efficient or superior in any way to the manner of handling such matters by its sister courts elsewhere in the state. More specifically, there is no reason to believe that the courts where the other four actions are pending cannot and will not efficiently resolve the four assertedly common questions of fact, which, per CCP 403, must necessarily not be complex. While it is possible that different trial courts will reach different decisions on one or more of these questions, doing so in different cases involving different parties is not necessarily disadvantageous and may even lead to better decision-making at the trial and appellate court level.

Mr. Kraus to prepare a proposed form of order that is verbatim with the court's ruling and include the granting of relief under CCP 404.60 today.
